sections.paragraph:
Original: שׁקל

Transliteration: sheqel

Phonetic: sheh'-kel

BDB Definition:

  1. shekel
    1. the chief unit of weight or measure
      1. silver - 1/3000 of a talent and equal to 132 grains
      2. copper - 1/1500 of a talent and equal to 528 grains

Origin: from H8254

TWOT entry: 2454a

Part(s) of speech: Noun Masculine
